title: A process for cleaning biogas while producing a sulfur-containing fertilizer
abstract: In a process for producing a fertilizer, ammonia is extracted or stripped from a feedstock, the digester itself or the digestate coming from an anaerobic digestion process, and the ammonia is used in a process where it reacts with sulfur compounds originating from hydrogen sulfide or other inorganic or organic sulfur compounds coming from the anaerobic digestion process. Thereby, a fertilizer product containing nitrogen and sulfur is obtained
claims: Claims Claims : 1. A process for producing a fertilizer, wherein: - ammonia is extracted or stripped from a feedstock, the digester itself or the digestate coming from an anaerobic digestion process, and - the ammonia is used in a process where it reacts with sulfur compounds originating from hydrogen sulfide or other inorganic or organic sulfur compounds coming from the an aerobic digestion process, whereby - a fertilizer product is obtained, said product containing nitrogen and sulfur. 2. Process according to claim 1, wherein the nitrogen and the sulfur both originate from the feedstock of the biogas product . 3. Process according to claim 1, wherein the stripping of ammonia from the feedstock is done only by raising the tem perature . 4. Process according to claim 3, wherein excess stripped ammonia is exported. 5. Process according to claim 1, wherein the heat for the stripping process comes from an amine wash treating of the biogas from the digester. 6. Process according to claim 5, wherein the heat pro vided by the stripping process is used as heat input to the digester or in the pre-heating of the digester feedstock prior to feeding such digester feedstock to the digester. 7. Process according to claim 1, wherein ammonia and hy drogen sulfide are stripped in one process. 8. Process according to claim 7, wherein the stripping temperature is above 90-95Â°C, preferably up to 100Â°C. 9. Process according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the ammonia extraction/stripping is combined with sanitation of the feedstock. 10. Process according to claim 1, wherein the ferti lizer product is ammonium thiosulfate (ATS) . 11. Process according to claim 1, wherein the stripped ammonia is reacted with sulfur compounds originating from a stream rich in CO2 and Â¾S coming from an amine wash used for cleaning the biogas from the digester. 12. Process according to claim 11, where the ammonia from the stripping process is removed from the stripping gas by condensing water present in the stripping gas, and where the ammonia is absorbed in the condensed water. 13. Process according to claim 12, where further ammo- nia is removed by subsequently contacting the stripping gas with water whereby ammonia is absorbed in the water.
